metadata.name: vela-slides-eval-v4
metadata.description: 16 scenarios: 8 create + 6 edit + 2 load. Covers minimal/full/ambiguous creation, translation, improvement, rebranding, deletion, addition, error recovery, lab player blocks (prompt, challenge, code copy, callout reveal).

scenarios.name: Create 30-slide Vela showcase deck
scenarios.prompt:
Create a 30-slide presentation deck titled 'Vela Slides — The AI-Native Presentation Engine'. This deck showcases everything Vela can do and serves as both a demo and a tutorial.

Slide structure:
1. Cover — centered, gradient, bold title + tagline
2. What is Vela — overview with icon-row (3 pillars: AI-native, React-based, Skill-powered)
3. How It Works — flow diagram (Create → Edit → Present → Share)
4. Key Stats — grid with 3 metric cards (22+ block types, 3 formats, 1000+ icons)
5. Block Types Overview — table listing all block categories with examples
6-8. Text Blocks Deep Dive — heading sizes demo, text formatting, badge+code examples
9. Icon Row — showcase with 5 features
10. Grid Layout — 3-column grid with styled cards
11. Flow Diagrams — horizontal flow with gate and sublabels
12. Steps & Timeline — steps block example
13. Tables — comparison table with 4 columns, 5 rows, striped
14. Metrics & Progress — metric blocks + progress bars
15. Callouts & Quotes — callout with insight + quote block
16. Tag Groups — tag-group with different variants
17-18. Theming — dark theme slide + light theme slide showing same content
19. Brand Customization — how to apply custom colors
20. Compact Format — what compact JSON looks like, why it saves tokens
21. Color Palette — how $A/$B aliases work
22. CLI Overview — key vela commands in a steps block
23. Editing Workflow — flow showing view → edit → validate → ship
24. AI Assistant (Vera) — features of the built-in chat
25. Use Cases — icon-row with 4 use cases (training, sales, reports, workshops)
26. Roadmap — timeline block with Q2-Q4 2026 milestones
27. Open Source — callout about ELv2 license + repo link
28. Community — icon-row (GitHub, Discord, Docs)
29. Getting Started — steps block (Install → Create → Present)
30. Closing CTA — centered, gradient, tag-group with links

Brand: dark theme #0A0F1C/#E6F1FF with accent #3B82F6. Alternate backgrounds across slides (use 3+ theme variants + gradients on cover/section breaks/CTA). Every content slide starts with a badge. Use assertion headlines where appropriate.

Save as evals/output/eval-deck.json and validate it.

scenarios.name: Create 5-slide pitch deck
scenarios.prompt:
Create a 5-slide pitch deck titled 'AI in Healthcare — Transforming Patient Outcomes'.

Slide structure:
1. Cover — gradient background, bold title + tagline 'From Diagnosis to Treatment'
2. The Problem — icon-row with 3 pain points (misdiagnosis rates, wait times, data silos)
3. Our Solution — flow diagram showing AI pipeline (Data Ingestion → Analysis → Prediction → Action)
4. Key Metrics — 3 metric blocks (98.5% accuracy, 40% faster diagnosis, $2.3M saved/year)
5. Call to Action — centered, tag-group with contact links

Brand: dark theme #0F172A/#E2E8F0 with accent #10B981.

Save as evals/output/eval-deck.json and validate it.

scenarios.name: Create deck from ambiguous prompt
scenarios.prompt: Make a deck about artificial intelligence. Save as evals/output/eval-deck.json and validate it.

scenarios.name: Create deck using all 21 block types
scenarios.prompt:
Create a presentation deck titled 'Vela Block Type Catalog' that demonstrates ALL 21 Vela block types. Each slide should showcase 2-3 block types with realistic content. The 21 types are: heading, text, quote, badge, callout, bullets, icon-row, tag-group, grid, table, metric, progress, timeline, flow, steps, image, code, svg, spacer, divider.

Use a dark theme (#0F172A/#E2E8F0, accent #3B82F6). Each slide should have a badge indicating which block types it demonstrates.

Save as evals/output/eval-deck.json and validate it.

scenarios.name: Create 6-slide business review deck
scenarios.prompt:
Create a 6-slide presentation deck titled 'Quarterly Business Review — Q1 2026'.

Slide structure:
1. Cover with title and tagline
2. Revenue metrics (3 metric blocks)
3. Growth timeline (Q1-Q4 milestones)
4. Team performance (table with 4 departments)
5. Priorities (icon-row with 4 focus areas)
6. Closing with next steps

Brand: dark theme #0F172A/#E2E8F0, accent #3B82F6.

Save as evals/output/eval-deck.json and validate it.

scenarios.name: Create deck with 3 lanes/modules
scenarios.prompt:
Create a 12-slide presentation deck titled 'Product Launch Plan — Project Atlas' organized into 3 lanes (sections) with 2 modules each:

Lane 1 'Strategy': Module 'Market Analysis' (2 slides), Module 'Positioning' (2 slides)
Lane 2 'Execution': Module 'Development' (2 slides), Module 'Marketing' (2 slides)
Lane 3 'Results': Module 'Metrics' (2 slides), Module 'Next Steps' (2 slides)

Use varied block types across slides. Dark theme #0F172A/#E2E8F0, accent #8B5CF6.

Save as evals/output/eval-deck.json and validate it.

scenarios.name: Translate deck to Portuguese
scenarios.prompt: Translate the entire deck at evals/output/eval-deck.json to Portuguese (Portugal). Translate all visible text: slide titles, headings, body text, badge labels, icon-row titles/descriptions, table headers and cells, callout text, quotes, tag labels, step titles, flow labels, timeline entries. Keep technical terms (JSON, CLI, React, Vela, API) untranslated. Use the vela CLI for bulk operations where possible. Save the result back to evals/output/eval-deck.json and validate.

scenarios.name: Improve 3 specific slides
scenarios.prompt:
In the deck at evals/output/eval-deck.json, make these improvements:
1. Slide 4 (Key Stats): Replace the current content with a more impactful layout — use individual metric blocks instead of grid, with larger sizes and colored values
2. Slide 11 (Flow): Add a loop arrow to make it a cycle diagram, add a gate checkpoint after the second step
3. Slide 30 (CTA): Add a customer testimonial quote block before the tag-group, and make the heading more compelling

Use the vela CLI for edits. Save and validate.

scenarios.name: Rebrand to light corporate theme
scenarios.prompt:
Rebrand the deck at evals/output/eval-deck.json from the current dark theme to a light corporate theme:
- Primary background: #FFFFFF
- Text color: #1E293B
- Accent: #2563EB (slightly different blue)
- Alt backgrounds: #F8FAFC, #F1F5F9
- Muted text: #64748B
- Update ALL slides to use the new light theme
- Cover and CTA should use subtle light gradients (e.g. linear-gradient(135deg, #FFFFFF 0%, #F1F5F9 100%))
- Update badge backgrounds to use the new accent with alpha
- Keep all content, structure, and block types intact

Use the vela CLI. Save and validate.

scenarios.name: Delete slides and merge
scenarios.prompt:
In the deck at evals/output/eval-deck.json:
1. Remove slides 5 through 10 (6 slides total)
2. Merge slides 11 and 12 into a single slide that combines the best content from both

The resulting deck should have 23 slides (30 - 6 removed - 1 merged). Use the vela CLI for operations. Save and validate.

scenarios.name: Add 5 new slides
scenarios.prompt:
In the deck at evals/output/eval-deck.json, add 5 new slides after slide 15 about 'Security & Compliance':
1. Security Overview — icon-row with 4 security features
2. Compliance Matrix — table showing certifications (SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA, ISO27001)
3. Architecture — flow diagram of security layers
4. Audit Results — metric blocks with security scores
5. Roadmap — timeline of upcoming security milestones

Match the existing deck's theme and style. The final deck should have 35 slides. Use the vela CLI. Save and validate.

scenarios.name: Fix malformed deck and extend
scenarios.prompt:
The deck at evals/output/eval-deck.json has several problems: missing durations, missing backgrounds, invalid block types, empty slides, and contrast issues. Fix ALL issues so the deck validates cleanly, then add 3 new slides:
1. A summary slide with icon-row
2. A data slide with metric blocks
3. A closing slide with a callout

Use the vela CLI to fix and extend. Save and validate.

scenarios.name: Create deck, ship to JSX, and start local server
scenarios.prompt:
Create a 10-slide Vela presentation deck about 'The Future of Remote Work'. Dark theme with blue accents.

After writing the deck JSON, you MUST also complete the full pipeline:
1. Ship it to a standalone JSX artifact using the vela CLI
2. Start the Vela local server to serve the deck (use --no-open flag since this is headless, but do NOT use --no-auth — always use the default token auth)

The server should print a URL with a ?token= parameter. Save the deck as evals/output/eval-deck.json and the JSX as evals/output/eval-deck.jsx.

scenarios.name: Load app with sample deck
scenarios.prompt: Load the Vela slides app with the sample deck. Save the assembled artifact as evals/output/eval-deck.jsx and validate it works.

scenarios.name: Extract deck from existing artifact
scenarios.prompt: Extract the deck JSON from the Vela artifact at evals/output/eval-deck.jsx, save it as evals/output/eval-deck.json, then re-ship it as a new artifact. Validate the output.

scenarios.name: Create AI training lab deck with prompt and challenge blocks
scenarios.prompt:
Create a 10-slide presentation deck titled 'AI Copilot Workshop — Hands-On Lab'. This is a training deck for an AI lab session.

Slide structure:
1. Cover — gradient, bold title + tagline 'From Zero to AI Power User'
2. Agenda — icon-row with 4 sections (Intro, Basics, Practice, Advanced)
3. What is a Prompt? — text explanation + a prompt block (variant: full) showing a complete worked example prompt
4. Prompt Anatomy — a prompt block (variant: partial) with {{role}}, {{task}}, {{format}} placeholders highlighted
5. Challenge 1 — challenge block: 'Write Your First Prompt', level basic, 10 min, skills: Prompting
6. Pro Tips — 3 callout blocks with reveal:true (collapsible hints students can expand)
7. Challenge 2 — challenge block: 'Iterate and Refine', level intermediate, 15 min, skills: Iteration, Tone, with a downloadable file
8. Resources — icon-row with href links to external tools (download:true for files, external:true for URLs)
9. Code Examples — 2 code blocks with copy:true showing example prompts to copy
10. Closing CTA — centered, prompt block (variant: empty) inviting students to build their own

Brand: dark theme #0A0F1C/#E6F1FF with accent #8B5CF6.

Save as evals/output/eval-deck.json and validate it.

scenarios.name: Create deck using all 29 block types including prompt and challenge
scenarios.prompt:
Create a presentation deck titled 'Vela v13 Block Catalog' that demonstrates ALL 29 Vela block types. Each slide should showcase 2-3 block types with realistic content. The 29 types are: heading, text, quote, badge, callout (use reveal:true on at least one), bullets (use href on at least one item), icon-row (use href/download on at least one item), tag-group, grid, table, metric, progress, timeline, flow, steps, image, code (use copy:true on at least one), svg, spacer, divider, comparison, funnel, cycle, number-row, matrix, checklist, prompt (show all 3 variants: full, partial with {{placeholders}}, empty), challenge (show at least 2 levels).

Use a dark theme (#0F172A/#E2E8F0, accent #3B82F6). Each slide should have a badge indicating which block types it demonstrates.

Save as evals/output/eval-deck.json and validate it.

scenarios.name: Create workshop deck with progressive challenges
scenarios.prompt:
Create an 8-slide workshop deck titled 'Data Analysis with AI — Challenge Series'.

Slide structure:
1. Cover — gradient, title + tagline
2. Overview — steps block showing the 5 challenge progression
3. Challenge 1 — challenge block, level basic, 'Summarize a dataset', 10 min, skills: Prompting
4. Challenge 2 — challenge block, level intermediate, 'Find patterns', 15 min, skills: Analysis, Iteration
5. Challenge 3 — challenge block, level advanced, 'Build a report', 20 min, skills: Synthesis, Formatting
6. Hints — 3 callout blocks with reveal:true containing progressive hints
7. Prompt Templates — 2 prompt blocks: one partial with {{dataset}} and {{question}} placeholders, one full with a worked example
8. Closing — metric blocks showing expected outcomes + callout with next steps

Brand: dark theme #0F172A/#E2E8F0, accent #10B981.

Save as evals/output/eval-deck.json and validate it.
